methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Layup Parts is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Zack Eakin that has raised $2M+. The target sector encompasses a broad peer universe of 9 private robotics and manufacturing firms plus 1 public comparable (277810.KQ), including companies such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the narrow peer set for seed-stage robotics lacks sufficient depth for reliable median benchmarking.

The valuation analysis applies a Stage-Progression methodology, which is the applicable triangulation approach given data limitations. Using the $2M raised as a proxy for current value and applying a typical 2.5x multiplier for seed-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $5M. The Peer-Median approach was not applied, as the robotics and manufacturing sector contains only 11 same-sector peers but none satisfying the filters of matching stage, adjacent stage, or comparable valuation tier—a threshold of at least three datapoints is required to avoid spurious benchmarking.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $4M to $6M (median $5M), derived from Stage-Progression methodology alone. Key uncertainties include the absence of traction metrics (revenue, growth rate, unit economics) and limited seed-stage comparable data within the narrow robotics vertical. A fuller picture would require visibility into capital efficiency, customer traction, or adjacent-stage funding multiples for similar robotics ventures.
